items.title: One run per training job
items.avoid: Starting multiple runs per epoch or logging unrelated trials into one run without structure.
items.prefer: Wrap each independent training attempt in one `start_run` / `wandb.init` … `finish` block.
items.why: Run ID is the comparison grain — mixing trials pollutes params and metric series.
items.example: Sweep 20 lrs → 20 separate runs, each with `log_param("learning_rate", lr)`.

items.title: Standardize metric and param keys
items.prefer: Team convention document plus shared helper: `log_val_metrics(f1=..., auc=...)`.
items.why: Comparison UIs pivot on key names — consistency is as important as logging itself.

items.title: Log reproducibility fields on every run
items.avoid: Logging only hyperparameters and final accuracy.
items.prefer: Add `seed`, `git_commit`, `data_snapshot_id` as params or W&B config on every run.
items.why: Without them, higher metrics may reflect data luck, not better models — see Reproducibility and Seeds.

items.title: Save artifacts for winning runs
items.avoid: Relying on metrics in the UI while model files stay only on local disk.
items.prefer: Log model, requirements, and evaluation plots as artifacts before closing the run.
items.why: Artifacts are the deployable output; metrics only rank candidates.
